title: The Four Language Pathways
subtitle: A developmental model of language, literacy, and signed-language writing
summary: Language input, language output, literacy input, and literacy output create a research-facing model for signed-language writing.
synopsis: Language input, language output, literacy input, and literacy output are distinct pathways in a research-facing model of signed-language writing. The model relates language acquisition, reading frameworks, testable expectations, language development, literacy layering, and signed-language writing without forcing those questions back into the public Foundations series.

slides.title: Problem Statement
slides.summary: Language development, literacy, and signed-language writing need a model that does not reduce language to sound or literacy to print.

image_prompts.concrete: Research model slide showing language input, language output, literacy input, and literacy output as four distinct pathways for signed-language development
image_prompts.abstract: Four developmental pathways opening from language into literacy

bullets: Language is not reducible to sound
Literacy is not reducible to print
Signed-language writing adds a new condition
slides.speech: This paper begins from a developmental problem. Signed languages require a model that separates language from sound and literacy from print. The Four Language Pathways gives that model a research-facing structure.
slides.notes: Open as a research model, not a finished outcome claim.

slides.title: Four Pathways
slides.summary: The model distinguishes language input, language output, literacy input, and literacy output.

image_prompts.concrete: Four-quadrant developmental model slide labeled language input, language output, literacy input, and literacy output
image_prompts.abstract: Four connected pathways balancing language and literacy

slides.speech: The model has four pathways. Language input is access to language. Language output is expressive use. Literacy input is reading or receiving durable text. Literacy output is producing durable text. Keeping these apart makes later research questions clearer.
slides.notes: This is the central model slide.

slides.title: Testable Expectations
slides.summary: The model creates testable expectations about early access, direct signed literacy, literacy output, and revision if the evidence does not support the claims.

image_prompts.concrete: Research expectations slide with four testable claims connected to future study designs
image_prompts.abstract: A model turning into testable research questions

bullets: Direct signed literacy should create distinct affordances
The model must be revised if evidence requires it
slides.speech: The value of the model is that it can be tested. It predicts that early language access matters, that direct signed-language literacy creates distinct affordances, and that literacy output matters. It also stays accountable: if durable signed text does not produce distinctive use affordances in tasks where durability should matter, the model should be revised.

slides.title: Research Questions
slides.summary: The pathway model leads into reading, recognition, syntax, spatial reference, education, corpora, and reusable infrastructure.

image_prompts.concrete: Research agenda slide showing pathways leading to reading recognition, syntax, education, corpora, and reusable research infrastructure
image_prompts.abstract: Four pathways branching into empirical research questions

slides.speech: The model leads directly into research questions: development and access, reading and recognition, syntax and spatial reference, education and institutional use, corpora, and reusable infrastructure.
slides.notes: Close by pointing to Reading Signed Text and the research program entry.

slides.title: What The Model Does Not Claim
slides.summary: The pathway model defines a research space; it does not claim that all mechanisms, outcome advantages, or educational questions are already settled.

image_prompts.concrete: Research model boundary slide showing four language pathways with open boxes for mechanisms, outcomes, education, reading, syntax, and empirical validation
image_prompts.abstract: A useful developmental model surrounded by clearly marked open research questions

bullets: Writing alone does not solve deprivation
The model should be revised if evidence requires it
slides.speech: The pathway model defines a research space. It does not claim that all mechanisms are already settled, that every outcome advantage has been demonstrated, or that signed-language writing alone solves early language deprivation. Its value is that it makes those questions clearer and keeps them testable.
slides.notes: Close the model with discipline. This is the guardrail that keeps the paper research-facing rather than triumphalist.
